sentenceBreakdowns: Họ (they) + uống (drink) + cà (coffee) + phê (coffee) + sáng (morning) + nay (this)
Họ (they) + mua (buy) + áo (shirt) + mới (new) + hôm (day) + nay (this)
notes_md:
**họ** = **they** (a group of people). It’s used for third person plural, when you’re not including yourself.

It’s neutral and works for any group: friends, coworkers, strangers. It doesn’t show age or gender.

Vietnamese often drops the subject if it’s clear, so **họ** may be omitted in conversation.

Stronger contrast: **họ** (they) vs **chúng tôi / chúng ta** (we).
exampleSentencesTranslated: They buy a new shirt today.

example.vi: Họ đang chơi bóng đá ngoài sân.

definitions.en: A pronoun used to refer to a group of people, usually third person plural.
definitions.vi: Đại từ dùng để chỉ một nhóm người, thường là ngôi thứ ba số nhiều.

notes:
họ = they (a group of people). It’s used for third person plural, when you’re not including yourself.

It’s neutral and works for any group: friends, coworkers, strangers. It doesn’t show age or gender.

Vietnamese often drops the subject if it’s clear, so họ may be omitted in conversation.

Stronger contrast: họ (they) vs chúng tôi / chúng ta (we).
exampleSentences: Họ uống cà phê sáng nay.
Họ mua áo mới hôm nay.
